rows.feature: Incremental / ECO global placement (freeze unchanged)
rows.notes: ECO placement freeze (0733f9c45b). Exposed a defect in its own right: -incremental did NOT actually freeze anything — it locked PLACED instances for the first pass, then called unlockAll() and re-placed the whole design. Frozen now means BYTE-IDENTICAL coordinates, not approximately unchanged (updateDbGCells carries the center as a float and the half-width subtraction truncates, so a round trip could land a dbu off). Paired negative: the same ECO WITHOUT the flag must move instances, else the positive gate is vacuous. GP4 genuinely required TWO changes, established by isolating them: keeping the locks ALONE does NOT give byte-identity — 252 of 291 frozen instances drift, so the coordinate restore is load-bearing. The drift is a separate confirmed defect (GPL-UPDATEDBGCELLS-DRIFT) affecting ANY caller relying on locked cells staying put. ITS SHAPE IS DIMENSION-DEPENDENT, NOT a uniform translation — an earlier characterization of '+162/+70 across all cells' was RETRACTED as a sampling error (five samples that happened to share one master, generalized to all 252). Measured per master: DFF_X1 w=6460 -> +162/+70; NOR2_X1 w=1140 -> -114/-280; MUX2_X1 w=2660 -> -93/-98; XNOR2_X1 w=2280 -> -1/-1; AOI21_X1 w=1520 -> -152/-280; AND4_X1 w=2280 -> -228/-280. A large subclass satisfies dx == -width/10 exactly with dy == -280; other masters break it. MECHANISM NOT IDENTIFIED. FOUR shape hypotheses were proposed and ALL FOUR falsified — recorded so nobody re-derives them: (1) uniform translation FALSE (8 distinct dy); (2) dy=-height/10 VACUOUS (the fixture is single-height, so a 'scales with height' claim is unfalsifiable by construction there); (3) dx=-width/10 FALSE (w=2280 gives dx in {-228,-1}); (4) dx determined by master FALSE at 97% (MUX2_X1 spans {-93,1308,1314}). No fifth hypothesis offered. Note the stable result came from an ISOLATION experiment while every retracted claim came from pattern-matching on output.

rows.notes: Per-scene clock skew (a39da3579c). SCOPE DISCLOSED, not over-claimed: skew is reported PER CORNER but the tree is NOT re-characterized per corner — TechChar::charCorner_ and the remaining cmdScene() sites are untouched, so characterization, wire RC and every build decision still come from the single command scene. These are the corner BEHAVIOUR of a single-corner-BUILT tree. Gate (a1ec29a37f) requires skew to TRACK an injected asymmetry: ff4 walked away in 4 steps -> 0.0000/0.0001/0.0004/0.0030 ns, strictly increasing, 319.9x dynamic range, each step equal to STA's own get_property arrival difference.

rows.feature: Per-layer metal density fill (dummy metal tiling)
rows.notes: Window-based per-layer metal density measurement + density-driven fill. MEASURED LIMIT, stated because it was found the hard way: -max_density guarantees the cap ONLY on the window grid the fill was driven over. A window straddling two separately-budgeted regions was never in the budget, so at a finer step the same design measures 0.400200 with 1 over-density violation against a 0.40 cap. The original 'post-fill peak 0.399998 <= 0.40' evidence was an ECHO of the budget constraint, not a measurement (DensityBudget::tryCharge rejects any shape that would exceed the cap, so re-checking that same grid cannot fail) — that sub-claim is WITHDRAWN. What stands, each independently falsifiable: density 0.250000 == hand-computed 2500/10000; boundary sharpness (0.2500->11 vs 0.2501->12; 0.2500->0 vs 0.2499->1); and an uncapped-vs-capped contrast reproduced on STOCK OpenROAD. The SAME grid-relativity applies to the MIN bound and is WORSE there (offset grid: 5 under-density vs 1 over) — see FL4. This row does NOT claim the fill produces density-compliant output; it produces output compliant relative to a grid the CALLER chose. MITIGATION (in src/fin/README.md): sign off at a step FINER than you filled with; a check on the fill's own grid returns 0 by construction and is not evidence. Corrected gate e21daa41a6 emits both numbers — over_cap_selfcheck 0 (labelled 'NOT evidence') and over_cap_finer 1 at step 50, the falsifiable one.

rows.feature: Multi-threaded OpenRCX parasitic extraction (per-net / per-window)
rows.commercial: Synopsys StarRC & Cadence Quantus distributed net-parallel extraction
rows.notes: OpenRCX (src/rcx) is entirely single-threaded; the coupling-and-compute sweep in extCC.cpp / extmeasure*.cpp dominates extraction wall-clock on large routed nets. Nets and GCell windows are largely independent given the geometry search structure, so add an OpenMP `parallel for` over the per-window CoupleAndCompute loop with per-thread local RC-accumulation buffers reduced at the end (same pattern drt already uses to thread FlexDR). Context: drt (11 files), grt (4), gpl (5), ant and OpenSTA (DispatchQueue thread pool) are ALREADY threaded — RCX is the one remaining serial backend stage, so this is the highest-value un-done OpenROAD parallel win.
rows.feature_zh: 多執行緒 OpenRCX 寄生萃取（每條 net / 每個視窗平行）
rows.notes_zh: OpenRCX (src/rcx) 目前完全單執行緒；extCC.cpp / extmeasure*.cpp 的 coupling-and-compute 掃描在大型繞線 net 上主宰萃取 wall-clock。由於幾何搜尋結構下各 net / GCell 視窗大致獨立，可對每視窗的 CoupleAndCompute 迴圈加 OpenMP `parallel for`，用每執行緒本地 RC 累加緩衝、最後歸約（與 drt 已對 FlexDR 平行化的手法一致）。背景：drt/grt/gpl/ant 與 OpenSTA（DispatchQueue thread pool）皆已平行化，RCX 是唯一還單執行緒的 backend 階段，故為 OpenROAD 最高價值的未完成平行化。
rows.reclass_note: [ALGORITHM-HARD] Not a localized change — needs a solver/data-structure refactor. Blockers measured: (1) ExtractOptions (src/rcx/include/rcx/ext_options.h:39) has NO thread field; no thread infra exists in src/rcx (grep std::thread|omp|tbb = 0; 'mergeParallel' in extmain.cpp is parallel-WIRE merge geometry, and SpefOptions.parallel is a distributed/streaming write flag, neit

rows.feature: Threaded PSM/IR-drop sparse factorization
rows.commercial: Ansys RedHawk-SC / Cadence Voltus distributed power-integrity solve
rows.notes: PSM assembles G·v=i and factorizes with a serial Eigen SimplicialLDLT. Static IR is rarely the flow bottleneck, and our added transient solve (solveTransient) reuses one factorization across all backward-Euler timesteps, so parallel factorization buys little. A real speedup needs swapping in a threaded Cholesky (CHOLMOD / Pardiso) — a heavy external dependency for modest gain on our block sizes. Defer.
rows.feature_zh: PSM/IR-drop 稀疏矩陣分解多執行緒化
rows.notes_zh: PSM 組出 G·v=i 後以序列式 Eigen SimplicialLDLT 分解。static IR 很少是 flow 瓶頸，且我們新增的 transient 解（solveTransient）在所有 backward-Euler 時間步間重用同一次分解，平行分解效益有限。真正加速需換成多執行緒 Cholesky（CHOLMOD / Pardiso）——對我們的 block 尺寸而言是重相依、低回報。Defer。

rows.feature: Distributed multi-corner / multi-mode STA (DMSA-style)
rows.commercial: Synopsys PrimeTime DMSA distributed multi-scenario STA
rows.notes: Within a single corner OpenSTA ALREADY parallelizes arrival/required propagation through a DispatchQueue thread pool (set_thread_count / Sta::setThreadCount). CORRECTION (2026-07-19 parallel-default audit): an earlier version of this note claimed the plugin 'already fans corners out as independent processes via eda_sta_mcorner' — that was INACCURATE; eda_sta_mcorner iterated corners in a SEQUENTIAL loop and set no thread count. Both are now being wired parallel-by-default at the CALL layer (sta -threads max per corner + concurrent corner fan-out). Cross-corner DMSA-style distribution as a FORK-internal feature remains low-ROI because the plugin/host is the right place to fan corners out (now doing so) — documented as reference, no fork work.
rows.feature_zh: 分散式多 corner／多 mode STA（DMSA 式）
rows.notes_zh: 單一 corner 內 OpenSTA 已透過 DispatchQueue thread pool 平行化 arrival/required 傳播（set_thread_count）。跨 corner 的 DMSA 式主機分散並非工具內建，但我們的 plugin 已用 eda_sta_mcorner 把各 corner 當獨立 process fan-out，故 fork 內做 DMSA 屬低回報重工。列為參考，不做 fork 工作。

rows.feature: Offset-robust density budget — guarantee BOTH bounds (min and max) at ANY window origin
rows.feature_zh: 偏移穩健的密度預算 —— 在任意視窗原點保證**上下限兩者**
rows.notes: Found while retiring a tautological gate on FL1, then WIDENED when the min bound was checked and proved to have the identical defect, WORSE. Measured with the check's own -limits_file (met1 banded, all other layers NO_LIMIT so they cannot contribute a count), filled to [0.30,0.40] on a 100/100 grid: on the fill's OWN grid 2 met1 windows / 0 violations (looks clean); on a 100/50 OFFSET grid 8 windows / 6 violations — FIVE UNDER-density against ONE over. THE MIN SIDE IS THE HARDER HALF: clamping a cap only limits HOW MUCH fill goes in, but meeting a minimum offset-robustly constrains WHERE it lands — nothing today controls where inside a window the metal sits, so a window can be topped up on one side and left sparse on the other, and an offset window sees only the sparse half. A foundry deck chooses its own window origin, so grid-relative compliance is NOT signoff compliance. GATE when built: both bounds must hold at several independent origins/steps; the existing known-bad cases (band_selfgrid 0, band_offset 6; over_cap_finer 1) are gated as OBSERVED values and must go green only by a real budget change.
rows.notes_zh: 退役 FL1 一個恆真 gate 時發現，隨後在檢查**下限**時擴大 —— 下限有完全相同的缺陷、而且更嚴重。填充到 [0.30,0.40]（100/100 網格）後量測：**自身網格** 2 個視窗 / 0 違規（看起來乾淨）；**偏移網格 100/50** 8 個視窗 / **6 個違規 —— 5 個密度不足、1 個超標**。**下限才是難的那一半**：夾住上限只限制「填多少」，但要在任意偏移下滿足下限，得控制「填在哪裡」—— 目前沒有任何機制決定金屬落在視窗內的哪一側，所以一個視窗可能一邊補滿、另一邊稀疏，而偏移視窗只看到稀疏那半。foundry deck 自選視窗原點，所以「相對於網格合規」**不等於**簽核合規。
rows.reclass_note: [ALGORITHM-HARD] src/fin has NO density CHECK at all (grep check_density|min_density|max_density|budget in src/fin = 0; DensityFill only STAMPS fill, it never verifies). The row asks for a fill that GUARANTEES min<=d<=max for ALL sliding-window offsets — the min side is the hard direction (an offset window straddling two grid-aligned windows can dip below min even when every

rows.feature: selective ungroup / uniquify boundary-opt (dissolve small/critical, keep replicated)
rows.notes: keep_hierarchy -max_instances <N> — the replication-aware 'keep replicated' half of commercial selective ungroup that stock yosys could NOT express (keep_hierarchy -min_cost is SIZE-only). Gap proved first: an 8x-replicated 1-xor leaf + min_cost 40 + flatten DELETES the leaf; -max_instances 1 keeps the boundary. Gate: combinational miter -equiv + sat -verify over an 8-bit multiply datapath -> 'no model found: SUCCESS!'; PROVEN-NEGATIVE (invert y bus) -> 'proof did fail'. Opt-in/byte-identical when unused (6/6 designs), isolated A/B over all 173 keep_hierarchy/flatten tests -> 0 mismatches. commit 8361cd614 branch vibeic/synth-fixes-w4.

rows.feature: Module-level parallel synthesis (DONE in fork) + residual ABC serial floor
rows.commercial: Synopsys Design Compiler / Fusion Compiler multicore synthesis
rows.notes: Module-level parallel synthesis is ALREADY implemented in our fork (kernel/threading.h — ThreadPool + ConcurrentQueue + DeferredLogs, consumed by opt_clean / opt_merge / abc / proc_clean / techmap passes). Do NOT re-add it. The remaining serial hotspot is ABC on a single large module: ABC is an external logic-optimization/tech-map engine that is internally serial, and per-partition ABC would need risky netlist partitioning for small gain. Reference only — no new fork work.
rows.feature_zh: 模組級平行合成（fork 已完成）＋殘留 ABC 序列地板

yosys.summary_zh: 合成 + ABC 技術映射（tech-mapping）。在正確性上最接近商用對標（模組層級平行合成、健全的 ICG LEC、datapath lift/prefix-adder 皆已完成）；在 QoR 上差距最大——最重要的未完成項目（timing-driven mapping、retiming、加法器架構自動選擇）由於 ABC 已內建對應演算法，僅需接線即可 fork 實作。

magic.summary: Batch GDS streamout + device/RC extraction + LVS-prep netlisting + edit-time DRC. Full DEF-fidelity import path landed (unknown-layer/NDR-via/zero-width guards, crash/hang trio, bridge-tech); top open item is foundry LEF/DEF layer-map auto-discovery, then a SPEF/DSPF writer.
